steps.title: Find the mode
steps.content:
The mode is the age with the highest frequency. Age 18 appears 6 times, more than any other age.
$$\text{mode} = 18$$

steps.title: Find the median
steps.content:
With 20 students, the median is the average of the 10th and 11th values when listed in order. Cumulative counts:
  • Positions 1–6: age 18
  • Positions 7–11: age 19
Both the 10th and 11th values are age 19.
$$\text{median} = \frac{19+19}{2} = 19$$

steps.title: Find the mean
steps.content:
$$\text{Sum}=18(6)+19(5)+20(4)+21(2)+22(1)+23(1)+30(1)=400$$
$$\text{mean}=\frac{400}{20}=20$$
A fast Desmos check is to expand the frequency table into a list, then compute the mean and median. The mode is still read from the highest frequency, which is age $18$.

steps.title: Order and select the correct choice
steps.content:
mode = 18, median = 19, mean = 20, so mode < median < mean.
  • A — mode < median < mean: 18 < 19 < 20. That matches choice A — A is correct.
  • B — mode < mean < median: would require mean < median, but 20 > 19. Wrong.
  • C — median < mode < mean: would require mode > median (19 > 19), but mode = 18 < 19. Wrong.
  • D — mean < mode < median: mean = 20 is the largest, not the smallest. Wrong.
